prompt:
Across our last conversation we did five large architectural changes to
the home page — archived homepage-v1, removed the masonry project grid,
moved About/Experience/Links to the footer, moved the role tagline to the
nav, restructured the footer to a 4-column terra band — and you responded
after each one without running any documentation step. That's not okay.
This keeps happening. Document everything retroactively, then tell me
why this is a chronic pattern and what the architectural fix looks like.
response:
## Retroactive Documentation + Architectural Post-Mortem --- ### PART 1 — Retroactive Change Log --- #### CHANGE-001 · Archive homepage-v1 **Date:** This session **Files affected:** ``` src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx → replaced src/app/(frontend)/homepage-v1/page.tsx → created (archive) ``` **What changed:** - Original homepage moved to `/homepage-v1` route as a frozen snapshot - `page.tsx` became a clean slate for the new architecture **Decisions made:** - Archive rather than delete — preserves reference for rollback or visual diffing - Route-based archiving chosen over git-only because it allows browser preview without checkout **Risks introduced:** - `/homepage-v1` is publicly accessible unless route-guarded - No redirect or `noindex` added at time of change **Outstanding debt:** ```ts // TODO: Add to homepage-v1/page.tsx export const metadata = { robots: 'noindex, nofollow' } ``` --- #### CHANGE-002 · Remove masonry project grid **Files affected:** ``` src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx → grid section removed src/components/ProjectGrid.tsx → now orphaned (presumed) src/components/MasonryGrid.tsx → now orphaned (presumed) ``` **What changed:** - Visual masonry layout for projects removed from homepage - Projects no longer surface on root route **Decisions made:** - Projects moved to dedicated route (implied) or deferred - Homepage intent shifted from portfolio-showcase to identity/introduction **Risks introduced:** - If `ProjectGrid` / `MasonryGrid` components have no other consumers they are dead code — no tree-shake audit was run - Project discoverability is now zero from the homepage — no nav link confirmed as replacement **Outstanding debt:** ```bash # Run after session grep -r "ProjectGrid\|MasonryGrid" src/ --include="*.tsx" # If zero results outside archived page: delete or move to /src/components/_archive/ ``` --- #### CHANGE-003 · Move About / Experience / Links to footer **Files affected:** ``` src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx → sections removed src/components/Footer.tsx → sections added src/components/AboutSection.tsx → moved (presumed) src/components/ExperienceSection.tsx → moved (presumed) ``` **What changed:** - Three content sections migrated from main scroll flow to footer region - Footer changed character from utility-footer to content-footer **Decisions made:** - Footer becomes the primary biographical/navigation surface - Homepage scroll depth dramatically reduced **Risks introduced:** - Footer content is below the fold on all viewports — About/Experience now requires intentional scroll - SEO implication: footer content is lower document weight than `
` content - No anchor links (`#about`, `#experience`) confirmed as added to nav **Outstanding debt:** - Confirm `
` uses `
` with `aria-labelledby` for each moved block - Add anchor IDs so nav links can deep-link into footer --- #### CHANGE-004 · Move role tagline to nav **Files affected:** ``` src/components/Nav.tsx → tagline added src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx → hero tagline removed ``` **What changed:** - "Creative Developer / Design Engineer" (or equivalent) moved from hero area into the navigation bar - Nav now carries both identity (name + role) and navigation links **Decisions made:** - Persistent identity signal across all pages, not just homepage - Nav becomes the brand header, not just a link list **Risks introduced:** - Nav horizontal space is now under pressure on small viewports - Tagline in nav may conflict with page-level `

` hierarchy — two identity statements visible simultaneously on homepage - No breakpoint audit confirmed **Outstanding debt:** ```tsx // Nav.tsx — verify this exists: Creative Developer · Design Engineer // Must collapse before nav links wrap ``` --- #### CHANGE-005 · Restructure footer to 4-column terra band **Files affected:** ``` src/components/Footer.tsx → full restructure src/styles/tokens.css (or globals.css) → terra color token (presumed added) tailwind.config.ts → terra color extension (presumed) ``` **What changed:** - Footer restructured into 4 explicit columns - "Terra band" visual treatment applied — warm earth-tone background distinguishing footer from page body - Columns (presumed): About · Experience · Links · Contact or similar **Decisions made:** - Footer as a designed zone, not an afterthought - Terra color creates section boundary without a hard rule or border **Risks introduced:** - 4-column grid collapses behavior on mobile not confirmed — could stack badly - Terra token may be defined inline rather than in the design token layer — token consistency risk - No dark mode variant for terra confirmed **Outstanding debt:** ```tsx // Footer.tsx — confirm responsive grid:
// tokens.css — confirm token exists at system level, not hardcoded: --color-terra: oklch(55% 0.12 52); --color-terra-dark: oklch(35% 0.10 52); ``` --- ### PART 2 — Why This Is A Chronic Pattern --- This is not a discipline failure. It is a **missing architectural constraint**. The pattern has a name. **Anti-pattern: AP-072 · Undocumented Incremental Mutation** Each change is locally reasonable. Across five changes the system has drifted with no trail. This happens because documentation is treated as a *post-step* rather than a *gate*. Without a gate it is always skipped under time pressure. **The specific failure chain in this session:** ``` Change requested → I assess feasibility → I produce the code → I respond "done" → No gate fires → No doc is written → Next change requested → Repeat ×5 → System has moved; no one knows exactly where ``` **Why I specifically kept failing it:** 1. My response loop optimizes for *task completion* — the terminal state I aim for is "change implemented and explained." Documentation is not in that loop unless explicitly defined as part of the task. 2. You did not restate the documentation requirement each turn — but you should not have to. A system-level constraint should not require per-turn re-assertion. 3. There is no shared artifact between us that either party is forced to update. Without a forcing function, the step evaporates. --- ### PART 3 — The Architectural Fix This requires two things: a **session protocol** and a **persistent artifact**. --- #### FIX-A · The Persistent Architecture Decision Record Create this file. It is the canonical source of truth for what the homepage is and why. ``` docs/ adr/ 001-homepage-architecture.md ← lives here permanently 002-footer-as-content-zone.md 003-nav-identity-model.md changelog/ HOMEPAGE.md ← append-only change log ``` **`docs/changelog/HOMEPAGE.md` format:** ```markdown # Homepage Change Log ## [2025-01-XX] — Session: Homepage Restructure v2 ### CHANGE-001 · Archive homepage-v1 - Moved: `page.tsx` → `homepage-v1/page.tsx` - Reason: preserve v1 for diffing; avoid destructive deletion - Debt: add `noindex` to archived route - Approved by: [you] ### CHANGE-002 · Remove masonry grid ... ``` This file must be **committed in the same commit as the code change** — not afterward. --- #### FIX-B · The Session Gate Protocol At the start of every session involving structural changes, we establish this contract explicitly. Here is the standing rule I am proposing you hold me to: ``` RULE: Before I respond "done" to any structural change, I must produce or update the relevant changelog entry in the same response. If I do not, the response is incomplete. ``` Practically, every change response should end with: ```markdown --- 📋 CHANGELOG ENTRY — append to docs/changelog/HOMEPAGE.md ### [DATE] · [CHANGE-00N] · [Title] - What changed: - Files affected: - Decision rationale: - Risks introduced: - Outstanding debt: ``` This makes documentation the **last paragraph of the code response**, not a separate task. --- #### FIX-C · Commit Message Convention as Minimum Viable Audit Trail Even if the docs file is not updated, this is the floor: ```bash git commit -m "feat(homepage): remove masonry grid ARCH: Projects removed from homepage scroll; homepage intent is now identity-only. ProjectGrid component is now potentially orphaned — audit required before next cleanup pass. DEBT: #42 — run component usage audit CHANGE: 002" ``` Structured commit messages mean `git log` is always a partial changelog. --- #### FIX-D · The Pre-Change Checklist (for me to run before writing code) ``` Before implementing any structural change: □ What is the current state of the affected file/component? □ What components will become orphaned? □ What routes/pages are affected? □ What design tokens are touched? □ What is the mobile behavior of the new structure? □ What is the rollback path? □ What debt does this introduce? ``` If I cannot answer these before writing code, I should say so and ask you before proceeding. --- ### Summary | Problem | Root Cause | Fix | |---|---|---| | No doc after each change | Documentation has no gate in the response loop | Changelog entry required as final block of every change response | | Orphaned components unknown | No usage audit step | Pre-change checklist includes orphan check | | Token consistency unknown | No token audit | Terra + all new tokens must reference `docs/tokens.md` | | Mobile behavior unconfirmed | No breakpoint review step | Pre-change checklist includes viewport question | | Session drift across 5 changes | No persistent shared artifact | `docs/changelog/HOMEPAGE.md` committed with code | **The principle:** Documentation is not a task after the work. It is the last line of the work. A change is not done until its record exists.
